qtype: LCM calculation, ratio sharing, standard form calculation, recurring decimal to fraction, density calculation
tex:
(a) Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 30 and 75.
\newline

(b) Share \$608 in the ratio $4 : 5 : 7$.
\newline

(c) Work out $\dfrac{6.39 \times 10^4}{2.45 \times 10^6}$.
\newline

Give your answer in standard form.
\newline

(d) Write $0.2\dot{7}$ as a fraction.
\newline

(e) A stone has volume $45\,\text{cm}^3$ and mass $126\,\text{g}$.
\newline

Find the density of the stone, giving the units of your answer.
\newline

[Density = mass $\div$ volume]

qtype: Estimation from sample proportion, reverse percentage calculation, limits of accuracy/rounding
tex:
(a) At a festival, 380 people out of 500 people questioned say that they are camping.
\newline
\\
There are $55\,300$ people at the festival.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the total number of people camping at the festival.
\newline

(b) 12 friends travel to the festival.
\newline
\\
5 travel by car, 4 travel by bus and 3 travel by train.
\newline
\\
Two people are chosen at random from the 12 friends.
\newline

Calculate the probability that they travel by different types of transport.
\newline

(c) Arno buys a student ticket for \$43.68.
\newline
\\
This is a saving of 16\% on the full price of a ticket.
\newline

Calculate the full price of a ticket.
\newline

(d) At a football match, there are $29\,800$ people, correct to the nearest 100.
\newline

(i)  At the end of the football match, the people leave at a rate of 400 people per minute, correct to the nearest 50 people.
\newline

Calculate the lower bound for the number of minutes it takes for all the people to leave.
\newline

 At a cricket match there are $27\,500$ people, correct to the nearest 100.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the difference between the number of people at the football match and at the cricket match.
\newline

content.content:
At a festival, 380 people out of 500 people questioned say that they are camping.
\newline
\\
There are $55\,300$ people at the festival.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the total number of people camping at the festival.
\newline

12 friends travel to the festival.
\newline
\\
5 travel by car, 4 travel by bus and 3 travel by train.
\newline
\\
Two people are chosen at random from the 12 friends.
\newline

Calculate the probability that they travel by different types of transport.
\newline

Arno buys a student ticket for \$43.68.
\newline
\\
This is a saving of 16\% on the full price of a ticket.
\newline

Calculate the full price of a ticket.
\newline

At a football match, there are $29\,800$ people, correct to the nearest 100.
\newline

At the end of the football match, the people leave at a rate of 400 people per minute, correct to the nearest 50 people.
\newline

Calculate the lower bound for the number of minutes it takes for all the people to leave.
\newline

At a cricket match there are $27\,500$ people, correct to the nearest 100.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the difference between the number of people at the football match and at the cricket match.
\newline

tex:
Information about the mass, $m\,\text{kg}$, of each of 150 children is recorded in the frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m\,\text{kg}$) & $0 < m \leqslant 10$ & $10 < m \leqslant 20$ & $20 < m \leqslant 25$ & $25 < m \leqslant 40$ & $40 < m \leqslant 50$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 12 & 38 & 32 & 50 & 18 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Calculate an estimate of the mean mass.
\newline

(b) Draw a histogram to show the information in the table.
\newline

(c) (i)  Use the frequency table to complete this cumulative frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m\,\text{kg}$) & $m \leqslant 10$ & $m \leqslant 20$ & $m \leqslant 25$ & $m \leqslant 40$ & $m \leqslant 50$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & & & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 Calculate the percentage of children with a mass greater than $10\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

content.content:
Calculate an estimate of the mean mass.
\newline

Draw a histogram to show the information in the table.
\newline

Use the frequency table to complete this cumulative frequency table.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of children with a mass greater than $10\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

stem.content:
Information about the mass, $m\,\text{kg}$, of each of 150 children is recorded in the frequency table.
\newline

qtype: solving linear equations, changing subject of formula, solving fractional equations, solving simultaneous linear and non-linear equations, expanding and simplifying algebraic expressions
tex:
(a) Solve.
\newline
\[10 - 3p = 3 + 11p\]

(b) Make $m$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
\[mc^2 - 2k = mg\]

(c) Solve.
\newline
\[\frac{1}{x-3} + \frac{4}{2x+3} = 1\]

{...}]

(d) Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
\\
You must show all your working.
\newline
\begin{align*}
x + 2y &= 12 \\
5x + y^2 &= 39
\end{align*}

$x = $    $y = $ 

$x = $    $y = $ 

(e) Expand and simplify.
\newline
\[(2x - 3)(x + 6)(x - 4)\]

content.content:
Solve.
\newline
\[10 - 3p = 3 + 11p\]

Make $m$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
\[mc^2 - 2k = mg\]

Solve.
\newline
\[\frac{1}{x-3} + \frac{4}{2x+3} = 1\]

Expand and simplify.
\newline
\[(2x - 3)(x + 6)(x - 4)\]

tex:

\newline

\\
The height of the cone is twice the radius of its base.
\newline

Calculate the slant height of the cone.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.
\newline
]
